title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Governor Maura Healey declared July 11, 2026, as 'Noah Kahan Day' in Massachusetts.
Kahan made history as the first artist to headline four consecutive sold-out shows at Fenway Park.
The Vermont-born singer is recognized for his music and mental health advocacy in New England.

title: ‘Let’s just call it a tour’: Judy Collins says her farewell tour isn’t a farewell
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Judy Collins, 87, is on her 'Sweet Judy Blue Eyes' tour, including stops in Cape Cod and Tanglewood.
Despite being billed as a 'farewell tour,' Collins clarifies it's not a final tour, similar to Bob Dylan's extensive touring.
The tour will be filmed for a documentary and runs until March 2027, featuring guests like Rosanne Cash and Richard Thompson.

title: 3 new movies to watch on Netflix in July
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix added 'A League of Their Own' (1992) starring Geena Davis as a women's pro baseball team player.
Brad Pitt stars in 'Moneyball' (2011) on Netflix, focusing on analytics in baseball management.
'Gone Girl' (2014) featuring Ben Affleck and Rosamund Pike is available on Netflix for streaming.

title: Red Sox win 7th straight game just hours after landing in New York
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox beat the New York Mets 6-2 for their seventh consecutive win, improving their record to 44-48.
Sonny Gray pitched six innings, allowing one run, securing his 11th win of the season for the Red Sox.
Weather and mechanical issues delayed the Red Sox's flight from Chicago, landing in New York just hours before the game.

title: Christian Gonzalez responds to report that he isn’t physical enough for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez responded on social media to Greg Bedard's report questioning his physicality as a corner for the Patriots.
Greg Bedard reported some within the Patriots feel Gonzalez isn't physical enough, sparking Gonzalez's social media reaction.
Gonzalez, a top NFL corner, had 69 tackles in 2025, close to Kamari Lassiter's 91, challenging perceptions of his physicality.

title: Here’s who experts have the Red Sox selecting with the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts provide diverse predictions for the Red Sox's No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Potential picks include players like Chase Brunson, Taylor Rabe, Bo Lowrance, Ace Reese, Cameron Flukey, and Zion Rose.
The 2026 MLB Draft starts Saturday, with the Red Sox holding 19 overall picks including first-round pick No. 20.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Long Walk,' a Stephen King adaptation, is streaming on HBO Max, featuring a deadly competition among teens.
'Project Hail Mary,' starring Ryan Gosling in a space adventure, is available on Prime Video.
'The Five-Star Weekend,' filmed on Nantucket and starring Jennifer Garner, is now streaming on Peacock.

title: A 4th Red Sox player has been named a 2026 MLB All-Star
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ceddanne Rafaela selected as a 2026 MLB All-Star, replacing injured Yankees outfielder Aaron Judge.
The Red Sox outfielder is having his best season with a .283/.330/.438 slash line and a .768 OPS.
Rafaela is the fourth Red Sox player named an All-Star this season, joining Contreras, Suarez, and Chapman.

title: Joey Chestnut to compete in inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Joey Chestnut to compete in the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship at The Brook Casino in Seabrook, New Hampshire on July 11.
Chestnut's personal chicken wing record is 242 wings in 10 minutes, while Geoffrey Esper holds the record with 281 wings.
Contest decides winner by weight of meat eaten, not number of wings; event starts at 5:30 p.m. and is free for attendees.

title: France-Morocco was a fitting end to a spectacular World Cup in Boston
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in the World Cup match held at Foxborough, marking Boston's final game as a host city.
A crowd of 63,811 witnessed goals from Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé, concluding Boston's month-long World Cup events.
Boston successfully hosted matches featuring top global teams, gaining international publicity and fostering connections among fans.
